titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of BL Cotton Ch. xviii.22

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=15 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:14:07.310177+00 | translation moa=16 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:14:05.226954+00

div.p:
. H . Comes . filius Regis Scot’. Episcopis . Abbatibus . Comitibus . Iust’ . Baronibus . Vicecomitibus . Ministris . et omnibus fidelibus suis . francis et anglicis . clericis et laicis tam futu
ris quam presentibus tocius terre sue salutem . Sciatis me concessisse deo et Ecclesie sancte Marie de hadintune . Clerchetune in perpetuam Elemosinam cum suis
rectis diuisis ex utraque parte aque sicut Willelmus de Graham et Durandus vicecomes . et Richardus . clericus et Osbertus prior de Edeneburc . et Malbeht de
libertona et Gillandres filius Oggu preierunt et Circuierunt . postquam Thoroldus Archidiaconus mecum finiuit apud pebles . et sicut antea mensurata
fuit . Concedo etiam predicte ecclesie unum plenarium Thoft iuxta ecclesiam in uilla de hadintona. et omnes decimas et rectitudines ecclesiasticas
de tota hadintunsira tam de Molendinis quam de aliis rebus . Volo itaque et firmiter precipio quod predicta ecclesia teneat et habeat prenominatam
terram clerchetune solam et quietam de me et de Thein et de omnibus aliis qui terram predictam tenuerint . de hadintune . de me et heredibus
meis et et de eredibus eorum . et omnes aliis rectitudines et consuetudines in decimis et Elemosinis ita libere et honorifice et quiete et plenarie sicut
aliqua Elemosina in tota terra mea melius et plenius et honorificencius et quietius potest dari et Concedi . Has autem prenominatas elemosinas do et
Carta mea Confirmo pro anima mea et patris matrisque mee et animabus antecessorum et successorum meorum . Testibus . Eng’. Cancellario . et Willelmo Mas
culo . et Edmundo filio Eimer . et Anselmo Nepote Iohannis episcopi . apud hadintonam .

Henry the earl, son of the king of Scots, to bishops, abbots, earls, justiciars, barons, sheriffs, officials, and all his sworn men, French and English, clerk and lay, future as well as present, of his whole land, greeting. Know that I have conceded Clerkington to God and to the church of St Mary at Haddington in perpetual alms with its right marches on either side of the the river just as William of Graham and Durand the sheriff, and Richard the clerk and Osbert the prior of Edinburgh [Holyrood], and Malbeth of Liberton and Gillanders son of Oggu led the way and went around, after Thorald the archdeacon determined the bounds with me at Peebles, and just as it was previously measured. I also concede to the aforesaid church one full toft next to the church in the toun of Haddington, and all teinds and ecclesiastical revenues from the whole of Haddingtonshire from mills as well as other things. I therefore will and firmly enjoin that the aforesaid church hold and have the aforenamed land, Clerkington, alone, and quit in relation to me and the thane and all others who shall have held the aforesaid land of Haddington of me and my heirs and of their heirs, and all other revenues and customs in teinds and alms as freely and honourably and quietly and fully as other alms in my whole land are well and fully and honourably and quietly able to be given and granted. These aforenamed alms I give and confirm by my charter for the sake of my own soul and of my father’s and mother’s, and the souls of my ancestors and successors. As witnesses: Engelram [Ingram] the chancellor, and William Maule, and Edmund son of Aimar, and Anselm nephew of Bishop John. At Haddington.
